preconditions: The original target bundle has already executed and may be translated
The replacement differs in one naturally aligned byte
The sequence performs fc.i, sync.i, and srlz.i before re-entry
inputs: A post-increment byte store patches the differing byte
expected_results: The store updates its base exactly once
A data load observes the replacement byte
Re-entry executes the replacement marker and writes 2
allowed_results: r14=patch_address+1, r22=replacement_byte, r20=2, and no interruption
cases.committed_prefix: The patch, base update, cache flush, synchronization, and serialization complete once before replacement execution
cases.restart_action: Not exercised for the valid mapped code page

oracle.derivation: The original and replacement bundle bytes are test-owned literal encodings; expected base, byte, and marker values are calculated without importing production decoder or translator tables

citation.anchor: read of the memory and ALAT state occurred after the update

preconditions: The RAM word is naturally aligned and mapped
The load and arithmetic consumer are dependency-correct
The translator may keep both bundles in one TB
inputs: The successor adds literal 9 to the loaded value
expected_results: No artificial memory exit changes architectural state
allowed_results: Exact two literal GR results and no interruption
cases.committed_prefix: The successful RAM load supplies the value consumed by its legal successor
cases.restart_action: Not exercised for the valid mapped RAM word

oracle.derivation: The initial RAM word and modulo-2^64 successor arithmetic are literal test data and a test-owned mathematical calculation

citation.anchor: The update is done after the load or store operation

preconditions: The store target is mapped and naturally aligned
The no-stop consumers form a legal group
Resident and forced one-bundle execution are both available
inputs: A same-group ordinary read of the entry base
A later load of the stored word
expected_results: Same-group ordinary source observes entry base 0x1800
Resident and inherited executions have identical GR/PR/NaT state
allowed_results: The exact declared base, entry-image, memory, successor, and equivalence results
required_unchanged_state: Issue-group entry image used by ordinary consumers
cases.committed_prefix: The store and one base update survive the final-bundle memory safepoint and later consumers
cases.restart_action: Not exercised for the valid mapped store

oracle.derivation: All address, update, stored, loaded, successor, and cross-mode expected values are literal test inputs or direct integer calculations

citation.anchor: At the point of exception delivery for a correctly formed instruction group

preconditions: The enabled update-form load occupies slot 1
inputs: Successful store/base, BR, PR, and GR prefixes
Slot-1 ld8 with register update and a NaT-tagged address
Poisoned load destination and base values
expected_results: Register NaT Consumption is reported at exact IIP/RI=1/ISR
All prefix effects are visible exactly once
Load destination, base, and address NaT remain unchanged
allowed_results: The exact saved interruption tuple and literal committed/suppressed state
required_unchanged_state: NaT-tagged address value and NaT bit
fault_priority: The enabled slot-1 load is the earliest faulting instruction
cases.committed_prefix: Every preceding effect commits once; the load destination/base and later effects do not commit
cases.restart_action: A repair handler would retry the saved slot-1 load without replaying the published prefix

oracle.derivation: The vector, slot, saved fields, prefix markers, poison values, and unchanged state are literal expectations derived from the cited commitment rule and test-owned instruction stream

citation.anchor: uncacheable I/O references are always presented to the platform in program order

preconditions: The Vibtanium I/O SAPIC MMIO window is mapped
Both MMIO instructions are non-final and force the I/O recompile lane
inputs: One successful prefix before each access
expected_results: Exactly one device write callback and one device read callback
The load returns the literal I/O SAPIC version
Each prefix and successor retires once
Each access has one pre-access/retry TLB classification pair
allowed_results: write_callbacks=1, read_callbacks=1, io_recompiles=2, MMIO load/store classifications=2/2, and exact GR results
permitted_state_changes: Load destination and declared prefix/successor GRs
required_unchanged_state: Unrelated guest memory and architectural state
cases.committed_prefix: Both prefixes retire once and the two MMIO transactions are presented once in store-then-load order
cases.restart_action: The implementation-internal I/O retry resumes at the access and is architecturally invisible

oracle.derivation: Device callback counts, access order, I/O SAPIC version, and all guest markers are literal test expectations independent of translator implementation decisions
